column1: *//* empty structures in the c file.
*//* Number of fields given should strictly match the number expected.
*//* Note that a trailing comma will be parsed as an extra empty field.
*//* NOTE:
*//* The minimal valid description of a SoC that the initial discovery
*//* test running on CXDT can work with has the correct number of DP entries.
*//* The test will output the discovered APS and COMPONENTS in this CSV format
*//* to be fed back into the sections below. Non-discovery tests will
*//* expect valid contents of the aforementioned sections.
*//* Section: DPS
*//* The data in this section describes the DPs in your SoC.
*//* Reminder: this is the only section that is mandatory for correct
*//* system discovery via CXDT.
*//* DP Number corresponds to the DP part of a component address.
*//* DPs numbering is expected to start from zero and be contiguous.
*//* DP Type must be a valid dptype_e enum value (defined in cx_enums.h)
*//* Target ID and Target Instance must be valid for Serial Wire Multi Drop DPs.
*//* Final AP must be the index value of the highest numbered AP attached to the DP.
*//* Max power up retries specifies how many times the acknowledge for DAP
*//* powerup is polled. Default value of zero will poll enough times for most
*//* systems. Increase this if you are seeing slow power acknowledge.
*//* Comment field should be present
*//* (including empty). Enclose the field in double quotes if you are using
*//* whitespace or commas as part of the comment.
DPS
DP Number
*//* Section: APS
*//* This section describes the APs in your SoC.
*//* NOT required for CXDT discovery test.
*//* DP Number corresponds to the DP part of a component address. The DPS
*//* section should have a valid entry for DPs referenced in this section.
*//* AP Number corresponds to the AP part of a component address.
*//* AP Type must be a valid aptype_e enum value (defined in cx_enums.h)
*//* Comment field should be present
*//* (including empty). Enclose the field in double quotes if you are using
*//* whitespace or commas as part of the comment.
APS
DP Number
*//* Section: COMPONENTS
*//* The data in this section describes all the CoreSight components in your SoC.
*//* NOT required for CXDT discovery test.

column1: *//* Section: System Memory Defines
*//* Some components
*//* processor's main address map as well as the debug addres map. The
*//* data in this section describes the base address and size of these
*//* components.
*//* The InstName in each line must be a valid Component InstName label
*//* (do not forget to double quote labels).
System Memory Defines
InstName
*//* Example entry:
*//* "CompoentLabel"
*//* Section: CPUS
*//* This section describes the processors in your SoC
*//* debug components within the procesor's address map
*//* components that are closely coupled to each processor.
*//* Each processor within a multi-processor cluster must be described.
*//* Base ROM Table instance should be the label for the ROM Table
*//* describing that particular CPU's subsystem.
*//* Arch version must be a valid cpuarch_e enum value (defined in cx_enums.h)
*//* System Address Mask must be a value that removes non-address bits (such as
*//* PADDRDBG31) when ANDed with a component memory address. Typically this
*//* is 0x7FFFFFFF for Cortex-A and Cortex-R processors. Zero is treated as
*//* a special case of no-mask
column2: so may be used for Cortex-M procesors.

column1: *//* System address must be the base address in the processor's memory map
*//* where the debug components are accessed. This is SoC specific for
*//* Cortex-A and Cortex-R systems
*//* All other cells in this section must be valid Component InstName labels.
*//* If there are fewer than 3 trace sources
CPUS
Base ROM Table Instance
*//* Section: TRIGGERS
*//* The Triggers section describes point-to-point CTI trigger connectivity.
*//* Driver Instance and Observer Instance must be valid Component InstName
*//* labels.
*//* Signal must be a valid signal_e enum value (defined in cx_enums.h)
*//* Port field specifies which port the connection describes for
*//* multiport components
TRIGGERS
Driver Instance

*//* Each line in the Trace section describes a list of trace components
*//* from Source through to Sink
